abstract: The object that defines a package product.

codeExamples.code:
let package = Package(
    name: "Paper",
    products: [
        .executable(name: "tool", targets: ["tool"]),
        .library(name: "Paper", targets: ["Paper"]),
        .library(name: "PaperStatic", type: .static, targets: ["Paper"]),
        .library(name: "PaperDynamic", type: .dynamic, targets: ["Paper"]),
    ],
    dependencies: [
        .package(url: "http://example.com.com/ExamplePackage/ExamplePackage", from: "1.2.3"),
        .package(url: "http://some/other/lib", .exact("1.2.3")),
    ],
    targets: [
        .executableTarget(
            name: "tool",
            dependencies: [
                "Paper",
                "ExamplePackage"
            ]),
        .target(
            name: "Paper",
            dependencies: [
                "Basic",
                .target(name: "Utility"),
                .product(name: "AnotherExamplePackage"),
            ])
    ]
)

---

crawled: 2026-03-10T20:12:01Z

# Product

**Class**

The object that defines a package product.

## Declaration

```swift
class Product
```

## Overview

A package product defines an externally visible build artifact that’s available to clients of a package. Swift Package Manager assembles the product from the build artifacts of one or more of the package’s targets. A package product can be one of three types:



The following example shows a package manifest for a library called “Paper” that defines multiple products:

```swift
let package = Package(
    name: "Paper",
    products: [
        .executable(name: "tool", targets: ["tool"]),
        .library(name: "Paper", targets: ["Paper"]),
        .library(name: "PaperStatic", type: .static, targets: ["Paper"]),
        .library(name: "PaperDynamic", type: .dynamic, targets: ["Paper"]),
    ],
    dependencies: [
        .package(url: "http://example.com.com/ExamplePackage/ExamplePackage", from: "1.2.3"),
        .package(url: "http://some/other/lib", .exact("1.2.3")),
    ],
    targets: [
        .executableTarget(
            name: "tool",
            dependencies: [
                "Paper",
                "ExamplePackage"
            ]),
        .target(
            name: "Paper",
            dependencies: [
                "Basic",
                .target(name: "Utility"),
                .product(name: "AnotherExamplePackage"),
            ])
    ]
)
```

## Creating a Library Product

- **library(name:type:targets:)**: Creates a library product to allow clients that declare a dependency on this package to use the package’s functionality.
- **Product.Library**: The library product of a Swift package.

## Creating an Executable Product

- **executable(name:targets:)**: Creates an executable package product.
- **Product.Executable**: The executable product of a Swift package.

## Creating a Plugin Product

- **plugin(name:targets:)**: Defines a product that vends a package plugin target for use by clients of the package.
- **Product.Plugin**: The plug-in product of a Swift package.

## Naming the product

- **name**: The name of the package product.

## Configuring Products

- **products**: The list of products that this package vends and that clients can use.

## Inherited By

- Product.Executable
- Product.Library
- Product.Plugin
